notes.anchor: since I had heard the temper of the assembly
notes.note: The contio was a non-voting public meeting at which magistrates addressed the people; its favourable reception of Pompey's published terms briefly raised Cicero's hopes that Caesar would accept and war be averted.

notes.anchor: in which he mocks Lucius Caesar's embassy
notes.note: Curio, now Caesar's man, ridicules the peace mission carried by L. Caesar; the legatio that had filled Cicero with hope in 7.16-7.18 is here treated by the Caesarian side as a sham, signalling the talks had collapsed.
